title: US civil rights agency seeks to toss demographic data collection | AP News
excerpt: The Trump administration is seeking to toss aside a 60-year-old requirement for tens of thousands of private sector employers to submit workforce demographic reports each year to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the agency responsible for enforcing anti-discrimination laws in the workplace. The EEOC’s Republican majority voted 2-1 Tuesday to rescind the data collection requirement, marking a quiet but profound salvo in President Donald Trump’s shake up of civil rights enforcement. The proposal will be subject to a 30-day commentary period before final approval.
content_html:
NEW YORK (AP) — How many women hold executive-level positions at top U.S. companies? What is the racial and ethnic breakdown of those roles? What is the gender and racial breakdown of the lowest-paid roles at those companies?

The Trump administration is making it harder for the public to know, moving to toss aside a 60-year-old requirement for tens of thousands of private sector employers to submit workforce demographic reports each year to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the agency responsible for enforcing anti-discrimination laws in the workplace.

The EEOC’s Republican majority voted 2-1 Tuesday to rescind the data collection requirement, submitting the proposal to a 30-day public commentary period before final approval. The proposal is a quiet but profound salvo in President Donald Trump’s shake up of civil rights enforcement, ending a practice that has endured through 10 Republican and Democratic administrations.

EEOC Chair Andrea Lucas, an outspoken critic of diversity and inclusion practices who has urged white men to come forward with discrimination complaints, argued that requiring companies to submit the annual demographic reports risks encouraging companies to justify discriminatory practices to diversify their workforce.

“It may promote racial stereotyping at work, and it may encourage employers to engage in discrimination,” Lucas said during a hearing ahead of the vote.

2 MIN READ

4 MIN READ

1 MIN READ

Former Democratic EEOC commissioners and civil rights organizations have denounced the proposal, saying it will deprive the agency of a critical tool for uncovering discrimination patterns and tracking how women and racial minorities have fared since the 1964 Civil Rights Act, which created the EEOC and prohibited employment discrimination based on race, color, sex, national origin and religion.

The EEOC, which receives more than 88,000 worker complaints each year, has historically used the data to guide its enforcement priorities and inform some of its investigations.

Commissioner Kalpana Kotagal, the sole Democrat left on the EEOC since Trump moved to wrest control of the agency, voted against the proposal.

“Today, the commission discusses whether to turn back time to a period before the civil rights movement, kneecapping its ability to protect workers,” Kotagal said.

Here’s what to know about the reporting requirement and the proposal to end it:

Sign up for Morning Wire: Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.

How the EEOC has collected the data

Since 1966, the EEOC has required companies with at least 100 employees, or federal contractors with at least 50 workers, to submit a form called the EEO-1 each year.

The form, which has evolved over time, identifies 10 job categories from “Executive/Senior Level Officials and Managers” to “Laborers” and “Service Workers.” It asks employers to report on the number male and female workers in each job category, as well as the number of workers from different race and ethnicities: Hispanic or Latino, Black or African American, Asian, Native Hawaiian, American Indian or Alaska Native and two or more races.

The data typically covers more than 50 million employees and 73,000 employers nationwide.

A view into gender and race in the private sector

The EEOC launched during the Biden administration an interactive tool allowing the public to explore historical demographic metrics across industries and job categories. The most recent data is from 2023. Under the Trump administration, the EEOC last collected EEO-1 data for the year 2024 but has not publicly disclosed it. Collection of the year 2025 would have begun this year.

The data shows white men dominating executive and senior manager roles at private companies, though women and minorities have made inroads, especially in the years following the #MeToo and Black Lives Matters movements.

Women remain underrepresented in the top ranks of companies. While they make up nearly half the workforce at the companies surveyed, women held just 34.5% of executives and senior manager roles in 2023. That was up from 29.2% a decade earlier.

White and Asian women made the fastest gains, and by 2023, were no longer underrepresented in senior roles compared to their numbers in the overall workforce. In contrast, Black and Hispanic women remained sharply underrepresented in executive and senior manager roles despite making modest gains.

Asian men have been proportionally represented in senior roles for years, while Black and Hispanic men remained underrepresented in 2023.

Of those demographic groups, only one was overrepresented in senior roles: white men, who made up a third of the overall workforce at the companies surveyed but held 52.7% of executive and senior management roles.

Why the government wants to end the data collection

Lucas said the annual reporting requirements impose “hundreds of millions of dollars” on costs on employers, a burden she argued was unnecessary absent “any allegation, indication, or evidence of discrimination.”

The move was recommended by Project 2025, the conservative Heritage Foundation’s blueprint that has guided many of the Trump administration’s policies.

A group of former Democratic EEOC commissioners and legal counselors said there is little evidence that companies are routinely using employment data to engage in quotas or race-based hiring.

“This is simply inaccurate and unsupported speculation, at odds with the ways in which this data is actually collected, managed and used,” the former officials said in a statement.

Instead, the officials said, tracking such data encourages companies to proactively examine their hiring, promotion, benefits and other policies to ensure they are not unnecessarily erecting barriers.

Kotagal said the agency has used the data to determine whether a discrimination charge might be part of a deeper pattern at a company. She cited a recent investigation of California supermarket chain Vallarta Food Enterprises, in which the EEOC alleges the grocer failed or refused to recruit, hire or promote non-Hispanic individuals. In that case, EEO-1 data showed that “nearly 100 percent of its employees were Hispanic,” Kotagal said. “It’s a key tool in our toolbox.”

The agency has also issued special reports on demographic employment trends across certain industries or roles.

Kotagal highlighted a 2024 report on the tech sector, which showed that women had made virtually no gains between 2014 and 2022, and that workers under 40 years old had actually lost ground. The report also found tech company workers were more likely that others to file age or pay discrimination charges, suggesting systemic barriers to women and older workers in the industry.

The report has been removed from the EEOC’s website.

Corporate retreat from demographic disclosure

The EEOC is prohibited from publicly releasing an individual company’s EEO-1 form, only publishing the information in the aggregate. In recent years, however, a growing number of the country’s top companies began publicly releasing their forms in response to pressure from shareholders and Democratic elected officials to show transparency in their diversity efforts.

Many of those companies have complained that job categories in the EEO-1 forms don’t align with their internal structures, a point Lucas echoed Tuesday, saying the categories don’t reflect the modern workforce. But advocates of EEO-1 disclosure argued it’s the only standardized form that allows for comparison across companies, and offer more detail.

However, the trend toward transparency has started to reverse.

Companies have started to pull back on publicizing both EEO-1 forms and their own diversity reports, which conservative advocates and the Trump administration have seized on to argue that companies are using discriminatory tactics to add women and minorities to their ranks.

In 2025, 24 companies in the S&P 100 — the largest U.S. publicly traded companies — chose not to disclose their EEO-1 data after having done so the year before, according to Andrew Jones, principal researcher at The Conference Board Governance & Sustainability Center. Still, 60 S&P 100 companies did release the data.

Meanwhile, fewer companies are publicizing any form of demographic metrics. The number of Russell 3,000 companies that disclosed metrics on women in the workforce fell from 75% in 2024 to 62% in 2025, according to a study by The Conference Board. Those disclosing metrics on minority representation fell from 30.9% to 26.5%.

Why companies may keep gathering data

Companies are still likely to keep track of their demographic data, whether or not they disclose any of those metrics publicly, and even if they are no longer required to submit annual EEO-1 reports. That’s because Title VII requires employers to keep records that could be pertinent to any discrimination investigation, and the EEOC is empowered to request them.

“What we are generally advising is to stay the course,” Jennifer Robins, counsel in law firm Saul Ewing’s Labor and Employment Group. “Private litigants, employment discrimination lawsuits are not going away, and this data is helpful to defending oneself.”

The EEOC has demanded extensive demographic data from companies to bolster Lucas’ own marquee cases. Those include an investigation into diversity, equity and inclusion practices of sports giant Nike, which Lucas has alleged discriminate against white employees; a lawsuit against the New York Times for allegedly discriminating against a white man who was passed over for a promotion, and an investigation into alleged antisemitism against staff at the University of Pennsylvania.

Lucas emphasized Tuesday that the EEOC would continue to demand data in the course of its investigations. Kotagal warned employers that under future leadership, the EEOC could reinstitute the EEO-1 collection.

Jocelyn Frye, president of the National Partnership for Women & Families, said she sees the elimination of EEO-1 data as part of an effort to obscure the prevalence of discrimination against racial minorities and women and create of a sense of urgency around protecting white workers from DEI-related policies.

But Frye said the data “doesn’t suggest that their top priority ought to be discrimination against white men.

“If the chair is moving forward with an agenda that thinks she ought to be focused on men and ought to be focused on white people, my answer is, ‘Well, does the data tell you that?’” Frye said.

___

The Associated Press’ women in the workforce coverage receives financial support from Pivotal Ventures. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP’s standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.

blocks.html_en: NEW YORK (AP) — How many women hold executive-level positions at top U.S. companies? What is the racial and ethnic breakdown of those roles? What is the gender and racial breakdown of the lowest-paid roles at those companies?

sentences.en: What is the racial and ethnic breakdown of those roles?

blocks.html_en: The Trump administration is making it harder for the public to know, moving to toss aside a 60-year-old requirement for tens of thousands of private sector employers to submit workforce demographic reports each year to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the agency responsible for enforcing anti-discrimination laws in the workplace.

sentences.zh: 特朗普政府正在让公众更难了解这一情况，并放弃了已有 60 年历史的要求数万名私营部门雇主每年向平等就业机会委员会提交劳动力人口统计报告的要求，该委员会负责在工作场所执行反歧视法。

blocks.html_en: The EEOC’s Republican majority voted 2-1 Tuesday to rescind the data collection requirement, submitting the proposal to a 30-day public commentary period before final approval. The proposal is a quiet but profound salvo in President Donald Trump’s shake up of civil rights enforcement, ending a practice that has endured through 10 Republican and Democratic administrations.

sentences.en: The proposal is a quiet but profound salvo in President Donald Trump’s shake up of civil rights enforcement, ending a practice that has endured through 10 Republican and Democratic administrations.
sentences.zh: 该提案是唐纳德·特朗普总统对民权执法进行的一次悄然但意义深远的一击，结束了历经十届共和党和民主党政府的做法。

blocks.html_en: EEOC Chair Andrea Lucas, an outspoken critic of diversity and inclusion practices who has urged white men to come forward with discrimination complaints, argued that requiring companies to submit the annual demographic reports risks encouraging companies to justify discriminatory practices to diversify their workforce.

blocks.html_en: “It may promote racial stereotyping at work, and it may encourage employers to engage in discrimination,” Lucas said during a hearing ahead of the vote.

blocks.html_en: The data shows white men dominating executive and senior manager roles at private companies, though women and minorities have made inroads, especially in the years following the #MeToo and Black Lives Matters movements.

sentences.en: The data shows white men dominating executive and senior manager roles at private companies, though women and minorities have made inroads, especially in the years following the #MeToo and Black Lives Matters movements.
sentences.zh: 数据显示，白人男性在私营公司中占据主导地位，尽管女性和少数族裔也取得了进展，特别是在“#MeToo”和“黑人的命也是命”运动之后的几年里。

blocks.html_en: Women remain underrepresented in the top ranks of companies. While they make up nearly half the workforce at the companies surveyed, women held just 34.5% of executives and senior manager roles in 2023. That was up from 29.2% a decade earlier.

sentences.en: Women remain underrepresented in the top ranks of companies.
sentences.zh: 女性在公司高层中的代表性仍然不足。

sentences.en: While they make up nearly half the workforce at the companies surveyed, women held just 34.5% of executives and senior manager roles in 2023.
sentences.zh: 尽管女性占受访公司员工总数的近一半，但到 2023 年，女性仅占高管和高级经理职位的 34.5%。

sentences.en: That was up from 29.2% a decade earlier.
sentences.zh: 这一比例高于十年前的 29.2%。

blocks.html_en: White and Asian women made the fastest gains, and by 2023, were no longer underrepresented in senior roles compared to their numbers in the overall workforce. In contrast, Black and Hispanic women remained sharply underrepresented in executive and senior manager roles despite making modest gains.

sentences.en: White and Asian women made the fastest gains, and by 2023, were no longer underrepresented in senior roles compared to their numbers in the overall workforce.
sentences.zh: 白人和亚裔女性增长最快，到 2023 年，与劳动力总体人数相比，担任高级职位的女性人数不再不足。

sentences.en: In contrast, Black and Hispanic women remained sharply underrepresented in executive and senior manager roles despite making modest gains.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，黑人和西班牙裔女性在执行和高级经理职位上的比例仍然严重不足，尽管增幅不大。

blocks.html_en: Asian men have been proportionally represented in senior roles for years, while Black and Hispanic men remained underrepresented in 2023.

sentences.en: Asian men have been proportionally represented in senior roles for years, while Black and Hispanic men remained underrepresented in 2023.
sentences.zh: 多年来，亚裔男性担任高级职位的比例一直很高，而黑人和西班牙裔男性的比例到 2023 年仍然不足。

blocks.html_en: Of those demographic groups, only one was overrepresented in senior roles: white men, who made up a third of the overall workforce at the companies surveyed but held 52.7% of executive and senior management roles.

sentences.en: Of those demographic groups, only one was overrepresented in senior roles: white men, who made up a third of the overall workforce at the companies surveyed but held 52.7% of executive and senior management roles.
sentences.zh: 在这些人口群体中，只有一个人担任高级职位的比例过高：白人男性，占受访公司员工总数的三分之一，但在执行和高级管理职位中占 52.7%。

blocks.html_en: Why the government wants to end the data collection

sentences.en: Why the government wants to end the data collection
sentences.zh: 为什么政府要结束数据收集

blocks.html_en: Lucas said the annual reporting requirements impose “hundreds of millions of dollars” on costs on employers, a burden she argued was unnecessary absent “any allegation, indication, or evidence of discrimination.”

sentences.en: Lucas said the annual reporting requirements impose “hundreds of millions of dollars” on costs on employers, a burden she argued was unnecessary absent “any allegation, indication, or evidence of discrimination.”
sentences.zh: 卢卡斯表示，年度报告要求给雇主带来了“数亿美元”的成本，她认为，如果没有“任何歧视指控、迹象或证据”，这种负担是不必要的。

blocks.html_en: The move was recommended by Project 2025, the conservative Heritage Foundation’s blueprint that has guided many of the Trump administration’s policies.

sentences.en: The move was recommended by Project 2025, the conservative Heritage Foundation’s blueprint that has guided many of the Trump administration’s policies.
sentences.zh: 此举是由保守派传统基金会的“2025计划”建议的，该蓝图指导了特朗普政府的许多政策。

blocks.html_en: A group of former Democratic EEOC commissioners and legal counselors said there is little evidence that companies are routinely using employment data to engage in quotas or race-based hiring.

sentences.en: A group of former Democratic EEOC commissioners and legal counselors said there is little evidence that companies are routinely using employment data to engage in quotas or race-based hiring.
sentences.zh: 一群前民主党平等就业机会委员和法律顾问表示，几乎没有证据表明公司经常使用就业数据来进行配额或基于种族的招聘。

blocks.html_en: “This is simply inaccurate and unsupported speculation, at odds with the ways in which this data is actually collected, managed and used,” the former officials said in a statement.

sentences.en: “This is simply inaccurate and unsupported speculation, at odds with the ways in which this data is actually collected, managed and used,” the former officials said in a statement.
sentences.zh: 这些前官员在一份声明中表示：“这完全是不准确且没有根据的猜测，与这些数据的实际收集、管理和使用方式不一致。”

blocks.html_en: Instead, the officials said, tracking such data encourages companies to proactively examine their hiring, promotion, benefits and other policies to ensure they are not unnecessarily erecting barriers.

sentences.en: Instead, the officials said, tracking such data encourages companies to proactively examine their hiring, promotion, benefits and other policies to ensure they are not unnecessarily erecting barriers.
sentences.zh: 官员们表示，相反，跟踪此类数据会鼓励企业主动检查其招聘、晋升、福利和其他政策，以确保它们不会设置不必要的障碍。

blocks.html_en: Kotagal said the agency has used the data to determine whether a discrimination charge might be part of a deeper pattern at a company. She cited a recent investigation of California supermarket chain Vallarta Food Enterprises, in which the EEOC alleges the grocer failed or refused to recruit, hire or promote non-Hispanic individuals. In that case, EEO-1 data showed that “nearly 100 percent of its employees were Hispanic,” Kotagal said. “It’s a key tool in our toolbox.”

sentences.en: Kotagal said the agency has used the data to determine whether a discrimination charge might be part of a deeper pattern at a company.
sentences.zh: 科塔加尔表示，该机构已利用这些数据来确定歧视指控是否可能是公司更深层次模式的一部分。

sentences.en: She cited a recent investigation of California supermarket chain Vallarta Food Enterprises, in which the EEOC alleges the grocer failed or refused to recruit, hire or promote non-Hispanic individuals.
sentences.zh: 她引用了最近对加州连锁超市 Vallarta Food Enterprises 进行的一项调查，平等就业机会委员会在调查中称，该杂货店未能或拒绝招募、雇用或提拔非西班牙裔人士。

sentences.en: In that case, EEO-1 data showed that “nearly 100 percent of its employees were Hispanic,” Kotagal said.
sentences.zh: Kotagal 表示，在这种情况下，EEO-1 数据显示“其员工几乎 100% 是西班牙裔”。

sentences.en: “It’s a key tool in our toolbox.”
sentences.zh: “这是我们工具箱中的关键工具。”

blocks.html_en: The agency has also issued special reports on demographic employment trends across certain industries or roles.

sentences.en: The agency has also issued special reports on demographic employment trends across certain industries or roles.
sentences.zh: 该机构还发布了有关某些行业或角色的人口就业趋势的特别报告。

blocks.html_en: Kotagal highlighted a 2024 report on the tech sector, which showed that women had made virtually no gains between 2014 and 2022, and that workers under 40 years old had actually lost ground. The report also found tech company workers were more likely that others to file age or pay discrimination charges, suggesting systemic barriers to women and older workers in the industry.

sentences.en: Kotagal highlighted a 2024 report on the tech sector, which showed that women had made virtually no gains between 2014 and 2022, and that workers under 40 years old had actually lost ground.
sentences.zh: Kotagal 强调了一份 2024 年科技行业报告，该报告显示，2014 年至 2022 年间，女性几乎没有取得任何进展，40 岁以下的工人实际上已经失去了地位。

sentences.en: The report also found tech company workers were more likely that others to file age or pay discrimination charges, suggesting systemic barriers to women and older workers in the industry.
sentences.zh: 该报告还发现，科技公司员工比其他人更有可能提出年龄歧视指控或支付歧视费用，这表明该行业对女性和老年员工存在系统性障碍。

blocks.html_en: The report has been removed from the EEOC’s website.

sentences.en: The report has been removed from the EEOC’s website.
sentences.zh: 该报告已从平等就业机会委员会的网站上删除。

blocks.html_en: Corporate retreat from demographic disclosure

sentences.en: Corporate retreat from demographic disclosure
sentences.zh: 企业不再披露人口统计数据

blocks.html_en: The EEOC is prohibited from publicly releasing an individual company’s EEO-1 form, only publishing the information in the aggregate. In recent years, however, a growing number of the country’s top companies began publicly releasing their forms in response to pressure from shareholders and Democratic elected officials to show transparency in their diversity efforts.

sentences.en: The EEOC is prohibited from publicly releasing an individual company’s EEO-1 form, only publishing the information in the aggregate.
sentences.zh: 平等就业机会委员会禁止公开发布个别公司的 EEO-1 表格，仅发布汇总信息。

sentences.en: In recent years, however, a growing number of the country’s top companies began publicly releasing their forms in response to pressure from shareholders and Democratic elected officials to show transparency in their diversity efforts.
sentences.zh: 然而，近年来，越来越多的美国顶级公司开始公开发布其表格，以回应股东和民主党民选官员的压力，要求其在多元化工作中表现出透明度。

blocks.html_en: Many of those companies have complained that job categories in the EEO-1 forms don’t align with their internal structures, a point Lucas echoed Tuesday, saying the categories don’t reflect the modern workforce. But advocates of EEO-1 disclosure argued it’s the only standardized form that allows for comparison across companies, and offer more detail.

sentences.en: Many of those companies have complained that job categories in the EEO-1 forms don’t align with their internal structures, a point Lucas echoed Tuesday, saying the categories don’t reflect the modern workforce.
sentences.zh: 其中许多公司抱怨 EEO-1 表格中的工作类别与其内部结构不一致，卢卡斯周二对此表示赞同，称这些类别不能反映现代劳动力。

sentences.en: But advocates of EEO-1 disclosure argued it’s the only standardized form that allows for comparison across companies, and offer more detail.
sentences.zh: 但 EEO-1 披露的倡导者认为，这是唯一允许在公司之间进行比较并提供更多细节的标准化形式。

blocks.html_en: However, the trend toward transparency has started to reverse.

sentences.en: However, the trend toward transparency has started to reverse.
sentences.zh: 然而，透明度的趋势已开始逆转。

blocks.html_en: Companies have started to pull back on publicizing both EEO-1 forms and their own diversity reports, which conservative advocates and the Trump administration have seized on to argue that companies are using discriminatory tactics to add women and minorities to their ranks.

sentences.en: Companies have started to pull back on publicizing both EEO-1 forms and their own diversity reports, which conservative advocates and the Trump administration have seized on to argue that companies are using discriminatory tactics to add women and minorities to their ranks.
sentences.zh: 公司已开始减少公开 EEO-1 表格和自己的多元化报告，保守派倡导者和特朗普政府抓住这些报告，认为公司正在使用歧视性策略将女性和少数族裔纳入其队伍。

blocks.html_en: In 2025, 24 companies in the S&P 100 — the largest U.S. publicly traded companies — chose not to disclose their EEO-1 data after having done so the year before, according to Andrew Jones, principal researcher at The Conference Board Governance & Sustainability Center. Still, 60 S&P 100 companies did release the data.

sentences.en: In 2025, 24 companies in the S&P 100 — the largest U.S. publicly traded companies — chose not to disclose their EEO-1 data after having done so the year before, according to Andrew Jones, principal researcher at The Conference Board Governance & Sustainability Center.
sentences.zh: 美国大型企业联合会治理与可持续发展中心首席研究员安德鲁·琼斯 (Andrew Jones) 表示，到 2025 年，标准普尔 100 指数中的 24 家公司（美国最大的上市公司）在前一年披露了 EEO-1 数据后，选择不再披露。

sentences.en: Still, 60 S&P 100 companies did release the data.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，还是有 60 家标准普尔 100 指数公司发布了该数据。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, fewer companies are publicizing any form of demographic metrics. The number of Russell 3,000 companies that disclosed metrics on women in the workforce fell from 75% in 2024 to 62% in 2025, according to a study by The Conference Board. Those disclosing metrics on minority representation fell from 30.9% to 26.5%.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, fewer companies are publicizing any form of demographic metrics.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，越来越少的公司公开任何形式的人口统计指标。

sentences.en: The number of Russell 3,000 companies that disclosed metrics on women in the workforce fell from 75% in 2024 to 62% in 2025, according to a study by The Conference Board.
sentences.zh: 根据世界大型企业联合会的一项研究，罗素 3000 强公司中披露女性劳动力指标的公司数量从 2024 年的 75% 下降到 2025 年的 62%。

sentences.en: Those disclosing metrics on minority representation fell from 30.9% to 26.5%.
sentences.zh: 少数族裔代表性的披露指标从 30.9% 下降至 26.5%。

blocks.html_en: Why companies may keep gathering data

sentences.en: Why companies may keep gathering data
sentences.zh: 为什么公司可能会不断收集数据

blocks.html_en: Companies are still likely to keep track of their demographic data, whether or not they disclose any of those metrics publicly, and even if they are no longer required to submit annual EEO-1 reports. That’s because Title VII requires employers to keep records that could be pertinent to any discrimination investigation, and the EEOC is empowered to request them.

sentences.en: Companies are still likely to keep track of their demographic data, whether or not they disclose any of those metrics publicly, and even if they are no longer required to submit annual EEO-1 reports.
sentences.zh: 公司仍然可能会跟踪他们的人口统计数据，无论他们是否公开披露任何这些指标，即使他们不再被要求提交年度 EEO-1 报告。

sentences.en: That’s because Title VII requires employers to keep records that could be pertinent to any discrimination investigation, and the EEOC is empowered to request them.
sentences.zh: 这是因为第七章要求雇主保留可能与任何歧视调查相关的记录，并且平等就业机会委员会有权提出要求。

blocks.html_en: “What we are generally advising is to stay the course,” Jennifer Robins, counsel in law firm Saul Ewing’s Labor and Employment Group. “Private litigants, employment discrimination lawsuits are not going away, and this data is helpful to defending oneself.”

sentences.en: “What we are generally advising is to stay the course,” Jennifer Robins, counsel in law firm Saul Ewing’s Labor and Employment Group.
sentences.zh: “我们通常建议的是坚持到底，”索尔尤因劳工和就业集团律师事务所的法律顾问詹妮弗·罗宾斯（Jennifer Robins）说。

sentences.en: “Private litigants, employment discrimination lawsuits are not going away, and this data is helpful to defending oneself.”
sentences.zh: “私人诉讼、就业歧视诉讼不会消失，这些数据有助于为自己辩护。”

blocks.html_en: The EEOC has demanded extensive demographic data from companies to bolster Lucas’ own marquee cases. Those include an investigation into diversity, equity and inclusion practices of sports giant Nike, which Lucas has alleged discriminate against white employees; a lawsuit against the New York Times for allegedly discriminating against a white man who was passed over for a promotion, and an investigation into alleged antisemitism against staff at the University of Pennsylvania.

sentences.en: The EEOC has demanded extensive demographic data from companies to bolster Lucas’ own marquee cases.
sentences.zh: 平等就业机会委员会要求公司提供广泛的人口统计数据，以支持卢卡斯自己的重大案件。

sentences.en: Those include an investigation into diversity, equity and inclusion practices of sports giant Nike, which Lucas has alleged discriminate against white employees; a lawsuit against the New York Times for allegedly discriminating against a white man who was passed over for a promotion, and an investigation into alleged antisemitism against staff at the University of Pennsylvania.
sentences.zh: 其中包括对体育巨头耐克的多元化、公平和包容性做法进行调查，卢卡斯指控耐克歧视白人员工；针对《纽约时报》涉嫌歧视一名未获得晋升的白人的诉讼，以及对宾夕法尼亚大学员工涉嫌反犹太主义的调查。

blocks.html_en: Lucas emphasized Tuesday that the EEOC would continue to demand data in the course of its investigations. Kotagal warned employers that under future leadership, the EEOC could reinstitute the EEO-1 collection.

sentences.en: Lucas emphasized Tuesday that the EEOC would continue to demand data in the course of its investigations.
sentences.zh: 卢卡斯周二强调，平等就业机会委员会将在调查过程中继续要求提供数据。

sentences.en: Kotagal warned employers that under future leadership, the EEOC could reinstitute the EEO-1 collection.
sentences.zh: Kotagal 警告雇主，在未来的领导下，平等就业机会委员会可能会重新建立 EEO-1 集合。

blocks.html_en: Jocelyn Frye, president of the National Partnership for Women & Families, said she sees the elimination of EEO-1 data as part of an effort to obscure the prevalence of discrimination against racial minorities and women and create of a sense of urgency around protecting white workers from DEI-related policies.

sentences.en: Jocelyn Frye, president of the National Partnership for Women & Families, said she sees the elimination of EEO-1 data as part of an effort to obscure the prevalence of discrimination against racial minorities and women and create of a sense of urgency around protecting white workers from DEI-related policies.
sentences.zh: 全国妇女与家庭伙伴关系主席 Jocelyn Frye 表示，她认为消除 EEO-1 数据是为了掩盖对少数族裔和妇女普遍存在的歧视，并营造一种保护白人工人免受 DEI 相关政策影响的紧迫感。

blocks.html_en: But Frye said the data “doesn’t suggest that their top priority ought to be discrimination against white men.

sentences.en: But Frye said the data “doesn’t suggest that their top priority ought to be discrimination against white men.
sentences.zh: 但弗莱表示，数据“并不表明他们的首要任务应该是歧视白人。

blocks.html_en: “If the chair is moving forward with an agenda that thinks she ought to be focused on men and ought to be focused on white people, my answer is, ‘Well, does the data tell you that?’” Frye said.

sentences.en: “If the chair is moving forward with an agenda that thinks she ought to be focused on men and ought to be focused on white people, my answer is, ‘Well, does the data tell you that?’” Frye said.
sentences.zh: “如果主席正在推进一项议程，认为她应该关注男性并且应该关注白人，我的回答是，‘好吧，数据告诉你这一点了吗？’”弗莱说。

blocks.html_en: The Associated Press’ women in the workforce coverage receives financial support from Pivotal Ventures. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP’s standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.

sentences.en: The Associated Press’ women in the workforce coverage receives financial support from Pivotal Ventures.
sentences.zh: 美联社的女性劳动力报道获得了 Pivotal Ventures 的财政支持。

sentences.en: AP is solely responsible for all content.
sentences.zh: 美联社对所有内容全权负责。

sentences.en: Find AP’s standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.
sentences.zh: 在 AP.org 上查找美联社与慈善机构合作的标准、支持者名单和资助覆盖范围。

content_html_zh:
纽约（美联社）——有多少女性在美国顶级公司担任高管职位？ 这些角色的种族和民族细分是什么？ 这些公司中薪酬最低的职位的性别和种族分布是怎样的？

特朗普政府正在让公众更难了解这一情况，并放弃了已有 60 年历史的要求数万名私营部门雇主每年向平等就业机会委员会提交劳动力人口统计报告的要求，该委员会负责在工作场所执行反歧视法。

平等就业机会委员会的共和党多数派周二以 2 比 1 投票决定取消数据收集要求，并在最终批准之前将该提案提交给为期 30 天的公众评议期。 该提案是唐纳德·特朗普总统对民权执法进行的一次悄然但意义深远的一击，结束了历经十届共和党和民主党政府的做法。

平等就业机会委员会主席安德里亚·卢卡斯（Andrea Lucas）直言不讳地批评多样性和包容性做法，他敦促白人提出歧视投诉，他认为，要求公司提交年度人口统计报告可能会鼓励公司为实现劳动力多元化而为歧视性做法辩护。

卢卡斯在投票前的听证会上表示：“这可能会加剧工作中的种族成见，并可能鼓励雇主进行歧视。”

2 分钟阅读

4 分钟阅读

1 分钟阅读

前民主党平等就业机会委员会委员和民权组织谴责了该提案，称该提案将剥夺该机构自 1964 年《民权法案》以来发现歧视模式和追踪妇女和少数族裔表现的重要工具。该法案创建了平等就业机会委员会，禁止基于种族、肤色、性别、国籍和宗教的就业歧视。

平等就业机会委员会每年收到超过 88,000 份工人投诉，历来使用这些数据来指导其执法优先事项并为一些调查提供信息。

自特朗普夺取平等就业机会委员会控制权以来，委员卡尔帕纳·科塔加尔 (Kalpana Kotagal) 是唯一一位留在平等就业机会委员会的民主党人，他对该提案投了反对票。

“今天，委员会讨论是否将时间倒回民权运动之前的时期，削弱其保护工人的能力，”科塔加尔说。

以下是有关报告要求和终止报告要求的建议的信息：

订阅 Morning Wire：我们的旗舰时事通讯详细介绍了当天的头条新闻。

平等就业机会委员会如何收集数据

自 1966 年以来，平等就业机会委员会要求拥有至少 100 名员工的公司或拥有至少 50 名员工的联邦承包商每年提交一份名为 EEO-1 的表格。

该表格随着时间的推移而不断演变，确定了从“行政/高级官员和经理”到“劳动者”和“服务人员”的 10 个工作类别。 它要求雇主报告每个工作类别的男性和女性工人数量，以及来自不同种族和民族的工人数量：西班牙裔或拉丁裔、黑人或非裔美国人、亚洲人、夏威夷原住民、美洲印第安人或阿拉斯加原住民以及两个或多个种族。

这些数据通常覆盖全国超过 5000 万名雇员和 73,000 名雇主。

对私营部门性别和种族的看法

平等就业机会委员会在拜登执政期间推出了一款互动工具，允许公众探索跨行业和工作类别的历史人口统计指标。 最新数据来自 2023 年。 在特朗普政府执政期间，平等就业机会委员会上次收集了 2024 年的 EEO-1 数据，但尚未公开披露。 2025 年的收藏将于今年开始。

数据显示，白人男性在私营公司中占据主导地位，尽管女性和少数族裔也取得了进展，特别是在“#MeToo”和“黑人的命也是命”运动之后的几年里。

女性在公司高层中的代表性仍然不足。 尽管女性占受访公司员工总数的近一半，但到 2023 年，女性仅占高管和高级经理职位的 34.5%。 这一比例高于十年前的 29.2%。

白人和亚裔女性增长最快，到 2023 年，与劳动力总体人数相比，担任高级职位的女性人数不再不足。 相比之下，黑人和西班牙裔女性在执行和高级经理职位上的比例仍然严重不足，尽管增幅不大。

多年来，亚裔男性担任高级职位的比例一直很高，而黑人和西班牙裔男性的比例到 2023 年仍然不足。

在这些人口群体中，只有一个人担任高级职位的比例过高：白人男性，占受访公司员工总数的三分之一，但在执行和高级管理职位中占 52.7%。

为什么政府要结束数据收集

卢卡斯表示，年度报告要求给雇主带来了“数亿美元”的成本，她认为，如果没有“任何歧视指控、迹象或证据”，这种负担是不必要的。

此举是由保守派传统基金会的“2025计划”建议的，该蓝图指导了特朗普政府的许多政策。

一群前民主党平等就业机会委员和法律顾问表示，几乎没有证据表明公司经常使用就业数据来进行配额或基于种族的招聘。

这些前官员在一份声明中表示：“这完全是不准确且没有根据的猜测，与这些数据的实际收集、管理和使用方式不一致。”

官员们表示，相反，跟踪此类数据会鼓励企业主动检查其招聘、晋升、福利和其他政策，以确保它们不会设置不必要的障碍。

科塔加尔表示，该机构已利用这些数据来确定歧视指控是否可能是公司更深层次模式的一部分。 她引用了最近对加州连锁超市 Vallarta Food Enterprises 进行的一项调查，平等就业机会委员会在调查中称，该杂货店未能或拒绝招募、雇用或提拔非西班牙裔人士。 Kotagal 表示，在这种情况下，EEO-1 数据显示“其员工几乎 100% 是西班牙裔”。 “这是我们工具箱中的关键工具。”

该机构还发布了有关某些行业或角色的人口就业趋势的特别报告。

Kotagal 强调了一份 2024 年科技行业报告，该报告显示，2014 年至 2022 年间，女性几乎没有取得任何进展，40 岁以下的工人实际上已经失去了地位。 该报告还发现，科技公司员工比其他人更有可能提出年龄歧视指控或支付歧视费用，这表明该行业对女性和老年员工存在系统性障碍。

该报告已从平等就业机会委员会的网站上删除。

企业不再披露人口统计数据

平等就业机会委员会禁止公开发布个别公司的 EEO-1 表格，仅发布汇总信息。 然而，近年来，越来越多的美国顶级公司开始公开发布其表格，以回应股东和民主党民选官员的压力，要求其在多元化工作中表现出透明度。

其中许多公司抱怨 EEO-1 表格中的工作类别与其内部结构不一致，卢卡斯周二对此表示赞同，称这些类别不能反映现代劳动力。 但 EEO-1 披露的倡导者认为，这是唯一允许在公司之间进行比较并提供更多细节的标准化形式。

然而，透明度的趋势已开始逆转。

公司已开始减少公开 EEO-1 表格和自己的多元化报告，保守派倡导者和特朗普政府抓住这些报告，认为公司正在使用歧视性策略将女性和少数族裔纳入其队伍。

美国大型企业联合会治理与可持续发展中心首席研究员安德鲁·琼斯 (Andrew Jones) 表示，到 2025 年，标准普尔 100 指数中的 24 家公司（美国最大的上市公司）在前一年披露了 EEO-1 数据后，选择不再披露。 尽管如此，还是有 60 家标准普尔 100 指数公司发布了该数据。

与此同时，越来越少的公司公开任何形式的人口统计指标。 根据世界大型企业联合会的一项研究，罗素 3000 强公司中披露女性劳动力指标的公司数量从 2024 年的 75% 下降到 2025 年的 62%。 少数族裔代表性的披露指标从 30.9% 下降至 26.5%。

为什么公司可能会不断收集数据

公司仍然可能会跟踪他们的人口统计数据，无论他们是否公开披露任何这些指标，即使他们不再被要求提交年度 EEO-1 报告。 这是因为第七章要求雇主保留可能与任何歧视调查相关的记录，并且平等就业机会委员会有权提出要求。

“我们通常建议的是坚持到底，”索尔尤因劳工和就业集团律师事务所的法律顾问詹妮弗·罗宾斯（Jennifer Robins）说。 “私人诉讼、就业歧视诉讼不会消失，这些数据有助于为自己辩护。”

平等就业机会委员会要求公司提供广泛的人口统计数据，以支持卢卡斯自己的重大案件。 其中包括对体育巨头耐克的多元化、公平和包容性做法进行调查，卢卡斯指控耐克歧视白人员工；针对《纽约时报》涉嫌歧视一名未获得晋升的白人的诉讼，以及对宾夕法尼亚大学员工涉嫌反犹太主义的调查。

卢卡斯周二强调，平等就业机会委员会将在调查过程中继续要求提供数据。 Kotagal 警告雇主，在未来的领导下，平等就业机会委员会可能会重新建立 EEO-1 集合。

全国妇女与家庭伙伴关系主席 Jocelyn Frye 表示，她认为消除 EEO-1 数据是为了掩盖对少数族裔和妇女普遍存在的歧视，并营造一种保护白人工人免受 DEI 相关政策影响的紧迫感。

但弗莱表示，数据“并不表明他们的首要任务应该是歧视白人。

“如果主席正在推进一项议程，认为她应该关注男性并且应该关注白人，我的回答是，‘好吧，数据告诉你这一点了吗？’”弗莱说。
